title: Lyonia compta
taxonomy: Lyonia compta is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to the southeastern United States, specifically in the states of Alabama, Florida, and Georgia. The classification of Lyonia compta can be further broken down into its kingdom and order, which are Plantae and Ericales, respectively. Lyonia compta is a shrub species that belongs to the genus Lyonia. This genus is part of the larger Ericaceae family, which includes other flowering plants such as blueberries and rhododendrons.
uses: Lyonia compta has been used in traditional medicine in various cultures, particularly in Asia, where it is valued for its potential health benefits. Traditionally, it has been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and stems are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. In addition, Lyonia compta is believed to have antioxidant and hepatoprotective properties, making it a potential treatment for liver and kidney diseases. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential health benefits.

properties: Lyonia compta, a shrub native to the southeastern United States, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. constituents: Lyonia compta contains active constituents including iridoid glycosides, which are known for their medicinal properties, and flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The specific iridoid glycoside composition of Lyonia compta may vary depending on factors such as the plant's habitat and growing conditions.
parts: Lyonia compta is a shrub native to Southeast Asia, and it has been used in traditional medicine. preparations: Lyonia compta is a plant native to Japan and other parts of Asia, and its leaves, stems, and roots are used in traditional herbal medicine. The plant's extracts are believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are utilized in various herbal preparations.
